name: Swapnil Prakash Lader
tagline: AI systems that are not just intelligent, but dependable in production.
oneLiner: Backend and MLOps engineer with 5 years of experience shipping LLM systems to production — scalable APIs, CI/CD automation, and self-healing infrastructure.
bio: I build AI-powered systems with a focus on scale, structure, and long-term reliability. As AI coding tools have evolved, my approach has shifted from build-first to design-first: AI makes code easy to generate, but it raises the bar for how well that code needs to hold up. I move fast without compromising system design — clean architecture, readable code, and infrastructure that keeps making sense as systems grow.
experience: Five years across backend engineering, scalable APIs, and MLOps/DevOps: batch pipelines processing millions of SKUs, an autoscaling chatbot serving hundreds of thousands of users, and CI/CD automation that brought a production-grade deployment culture to on-prem LLM workstations. I design for failure as a first-class concern — fallbacks, retries, and adaptive workflows that keep systems reliable under real-world conditions.

projects.title: Autoscaling Chatbot Platform
projects.blurb: Chatbot backend serving hundreds of thousands of users with autoscaling infrastructure.
projects.summary:
## Problem

Conversational traffic is spiky. A chatbot serving hundreds of thousands of users needs infrastructure that scales out under load and scales back when idle — without dropping conversations.

## Approach

Built the backend on autoscaling infrastructure with load-aware scaling policies, graceful connection draining, and fallback responses when downstream models are saturated. The system stays responsive under real-world load patterns, not just ideal ones.

projects.title: On-Prem LLM Deployment Pipeline
projects.blurb: CI/CD automation that deploys LLMs to office workstations with vLLM — production-grade deployment culture for on-prem AI.
projects.summary:
## Problem

Deploying LLMs to local workstations was manual and inconsistent — every deployment was a one-off, with no repeatable process and no visibility into what was running where.

## Approach

Built an automated CI/CD pipeline that treats on-prem workstations like a production fleet: versioned model rollouts served through vLLM, health checks, and clean rollback paths. Deployment time dropped from ad-hoc manual work to a predictable, orchestrated process — and it introduced a production-grade deployment culture across the org.

projects.title: SKU Batch Processing Platform
projects.blurb: Backend pipeline handling millions of SKUs through reliable, observable batch processing.
projects.summary:
## Problem

Catalog operations at millions-of-SKUs scale can't run on request/response patterns — work has to be batched, monitored, and safe to retry.

## Approach

Designed the batch architecture end to end: chunked processing with idempotent retries, dead-letter handling for poison records, and Grafana dashboards for throughput and failure rates. Failure is treated as a first-class concern — the pipeline self-heals from partial failures instead of requiring manual restarts.
